section.name: Core Identity (did:aip)
section.t: Every AIP agent has a globally unique, persistent identifier conforming to the W3C Decentralized Identifier (DID) standard [W3C-DID]. The AIP DID method is did:aip, registered with the W3C DID method registry.

section.name: AID Syntax
section.t: An Agent Identity Descriptor (AID) conforms to the following ABNF:
section.artwork: AID = "did:aip:" namespace ":" agent-id namespace = LOALPHA *( LOALPHA / DIGIT / "-" ) agent-id = 32LOHEXDIG LOALPHA = %x61-7A ; a-z only, lowercase DIGIT = %x30-39 LOHEXDIG = DIGIT / "a" / "b" / "c" / "d" / "e" / "f" ; lowercase hex digit only
section.t: The namespace MUST begin with a lowercase alpha character, MUST NOT end with a hyphen, and MUST NOT contain consecutive hyphens. Implementations MUST reject AIDs containing uppercase hex digits or uppercase namespace characters.

ul.li: namespace: A lowercase alphanumeric string (ALPHA followed by any combination of ALPHA, DIGIT, or hyphens) identifying the agent type. Registered namespaces include: personal, enterprise, service, orchestrator, ephemeral. Custom namespaces MAY be registered.
agent-id: A 32-character hexadecimal string (lowercase) computed as the SHA-256 hash of the agent's public Ed25519 key material (the JWK x value base64url-decoded).

section.name: AID Derivation
section.t: An AID is derived deterministically from the agent's Ed25519 public key:

ol.li: Encode the public key as a JWK with kty="OKP", crv="Ed25519", and the public key value in the x field (base64url-encoded).
Compute SHA-256(base64url_decode(x)) to obtain a 32-byte hash.
Hex-encode the hash (lowercase) to form the agent-id.
Combine with the chosen namespace to form the complete AID: did:aip:<namespace>:<agent-id>.

section.t: This derivation is deterministic and cryptographically self-verifying: possession of the private key is both necessary and sufficient to claim ownership of the AID.

section.name: Registered Namespaces

dl.dd: An AI assistant serving a single human principal. Personal agents declare this namespace.
An agent deployed by an organisation for use within the organisation.
A service-owned agent providing functionality to principals (e.g., payment processor, communication provider).

dd.t: An agent that spawns and manages sub-agents. Typically has spawn_agents.create and spawn_agents.manage scopes.

dl.dd: A short-lived agent created for a single task. Ephemeral agents MUST include a task_id in their Principal Token and MUST have a limited delegation_valid_for_seconds (typically < 1 hour).
An AIP Registry instance. Created via the Registry Genesis procedure. A Registry AID MUST use the registry namespace.

section.t: Custom namespaces MAY be registered with the W3C and the AIP registry. Namespaces are immutable once registered.

section.name: Agent Identity Object Schema
section.t: The Agent Identity Object is the base document for an agent. It includes the AID, public key material, and metadata. The schema is defined in Section 4.2.2 (see also schemas/agent-identity.schema.json).

ul.li: type: The namespace component of the AID (REQUIRED, MUST match)
model: AI model information including provider and model_id (REQUIRED)
public_key: JWK format Ed25519 public key (REQUIRED)
version: Identity version number for key rotation (REQUIRED, minimum 1)
previous_key_signature: EdDSA signature by previous key when rotating (REQUIRED if version > 1)

section.name: Uniqueness and Immutability
section.t: An AID MUST be globally unique. Once an agent is registered in a Registry, its AID is immutable. An AID cannot be reused or transferred. Two agents with identical public keys will derive identical AIDs, so the cryptographic relationship is deterministic.
